narrative:
DOMINANT DRIVERS

1. USD weakness because US growth disappointment compounds bearish USD positioning across all major pairs with H4 signals uniformly dollar-negative
2. JPY strength because confirmed BoJ intervention narrative aligns with strong H4 sell signals across all JPY crosses including EURJPY and NZDJPY at -5
3. CHF dominance because CSM ranks CHF at maximum 100 strength with USDCHF showing strongest sell signal at -5 confirming safe-haven demand

PRICE CONFIRMS

USDCHF strong sell at -5 and CHF CSM at 100 are the clearest confirmation of the dominant USD weakness and safe-haven bid narrative, with GBPUSD and AUDUSD both printing buy signals at +4 consistent with broad dollar selling. The JPY cross sell signals are uniformly confirmed across EURJPY, NZDJPY at -5 and AUDJPY, CADJPY at -3, with the +0.94 to +0.97 correlation cluster across JPY crosses meaning any one of them leading lower drags the entire complex. EURUSD is notably absent from strong buy signals despite EUR CSM at zero, suggesting EUR is the laggard in the anti-dollar move with GBP and AUD leading.

REGIME

Mixed conflicted. VIX falling 7.87% and S&P500 up 0.33% signal risk-on equity behavior, but Gold up 1.87% to 4630 alongside JPY intervention and geopolitical oil disruption risk confirm simultaneous safe-haven demand, producing a regime where both risk assets and havens rally against a collapsing dollar.

CONTRADICTIONS

1. VIX versus Gold: VIX dropping sharply implies declining fear while Gold surging 1.87% implies active safe-haven accumulation, these cannot both be risk-off in the traditional sense
2. EUR fundamentals versus CSM: ECB hawkish hold with June hike prospects is bullish EUR by news but EUR CSM sits at zero, the weakest in the complex, rejecting the fundamental narrative on price

TRADE IMPLICATIONS

Best: USDCHF down because CHF CSM at 100 plus -5 signal plus USD bearish fundamentals fully aligned - Conviction 9/10
Secondary: NZDJPY down because -5 H4 signal with 0.96 AUD/NZD JPY correlation confirms systemic JPY bid - Conviction 8/10
Avoid: EURUSD because EUR CSM zero conflicts with ECB hawkish narrative creating directionless noise
Watch: USDJPY breaking below the current H4 structure confirms JPY intervention holding and validates NZDJPY short as systemic rather than idiosyncratic

themes.theme: US growth disappointment and mixed data weigh on USD
ECB hawkish hold with June hike prospects strengthening
Japan FX intervention to support JPY against speculative weakness
BoE cautious hold with potential one-and-done June hike
Geopolitical risk from Strait of Hormuz shutdown and Iran blockade
Oil supply disruption risk supports ECB hawkishness and stagflation fears
CAD supported by steady growth but structural headwinds cap upside
AUD firm on hawkish RBA expectations
Gold supported by geopolitics and weaker USD but capped by high rates
Fed steady easing path contrasts with other central banks leaning hawkish

key_observation: USD under broad pressure as Q1 GDP misses expectations at 2.0 percent versus 2.3 percent forecast while ECB and BoE both signal potential June hikes and Japan conducts first FX intervention since 2024. The divergence between a softening US economy and hawkish-leaning G10 central banks is driving a clear anti-dollar theme. Strait of Hormuz disruption and Iran tensions add a geopolitical risk premium that further complicates the outlook via energy prices and stagflation risk in Europe.
watch: ECB June hike decision as Brent above 100 dollars creates stagflation dilemma. Japan intervention follow-through and MoF rhetoric. US May payrolls for confirmation of growth slowdown. Strait of Hormuz resolution timeline and oil price trajectory. Fed June meeting pricing given divergence from other G10 central banks.
